title: Metabolites, Lipids, Protein Extraction Protocol (MPLEx)
author: Heather Olson
materials: Once the lipid sample (or process blank) has been placed into the Waters autosampler vial, apply a breathable tube membrane to the top of the vial and dry sample completely in a centrifugal concentrator (e.g. Labconco Centrivap, Thermo SpeedVac).

steps.title: This protocol works best on samples...
steps.instruction: This protocol works best on samples where the volume is 200 µL or less. The samples can be biofluids or tissues placed in 4 ℃ MilliQ water (enough volume to cover the tissue). The protocol indicating how we pre-processed tissue blocks embedded in CMC or OCT is titled : " Preparation of Lung Tissue Blocks for MPLEx " For samples that have a higher volume (but potentially low protein concentration, such as Bronchoalveolar Lavage Fluid (BALF)), it is best to lyophilize to dryness and reconstitute with water. Using larger initial volumes will result in the metabolite/lipid extraction volumes being much larger, and the protein "puck" in the interphase will be thinned out in a larger tube and more problematic to deal with. The protocol can be scaled up or down, as long as the ratio of Chloroform:Methanol:Water is 8:4:3.

steps.title: Transfer metabolite layer from above process into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer metabolite layer from above process into a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube similar to the ones used for the MPLEx extraction process

steps.title: Select appropriate size of tube for tissue mass
steps.instruction: Select appropriate size of tube for tissue mass. For example, a small biopsy sample (100-200 mg) will fit in a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Much larger than that, you should put the sample into a 5-mL microcentrifuge tube for processing. Additionally, for metabolite/lipid normalization, it is a good practice to obtain the weight of each tissue before processing. Note: the weights can be used for potential normalization steps of lipid/metabolite data as no general assay enables to determine total lipid/metabolite concentration.

steps.title: For samples in a 1
steps.instruction: For samples in a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube, add 470 µL of ice-cold (-20 ℃) 4:3 Methanol:Water.

steps.title: Homogenize tissue/cell pellet with pellet pestle...
steps.instruction: Homogenize tissue/cell pellet with pellet pestle homogenizer (similar to ones used for DNA homogenization)

steps.title: Vortex all samples again for 1...
steps.instruction: Vortex all samples again for 1 minute, followed by centrifugation at 10,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4 ℃. Samples will be separated into 3 distinct phases following this step.

steps.title: Once all samples have been homogenized,...
steps.instruction: Once all samples have been homogenized, 530 µL of ice-cold (-20 ℃) chloroform is added (this will result in the 8:4:3 CHCl3:MeOH:H 2 O ratio mentioned above) followed by brief vortexing. Once chloroform has been added to all samples, vortex all samples at highest speed for 1 minute

steps.title: Place all samples on ice for...
steps.instruction: Place all samples on ice for 5 minutes following vortexing step.

steps.title: Follow steps 6-9 of the "Solvent-Solvent...
steps.instruction: Follow steps 6-9 of the "Solvent-Solvent Extraction of Liquid Samples" to obtain the metabolite, lipid, and protein phases from these samples.

steps.title: Apply a breathable tube membrane to...
steps.instruction: Apply a breathable tube membrane to the top of the vial and dry sample completely in a centrifugal concentrator (e.g. Labconco Centrivap, Thermo SpeedVac).

steps.title: Add 150-200 uL of 80:20 methanol:water...
steps.instruction: Add 150-200 uL of 80:20 methanol:water to dried sample in tube. Vortex and sonicate until sample dissolves. There may be residue that does not dissolve after vortexing and sonicating, do not spend more than 5 minutes on resuspension attempts.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 21,000 x g for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 21,000 x g for 10 minutes

steps.title: While samples are centrifuging, pre-condition a 1-mL, 0
steps.instruction: While samples are centrifuging, pre-condition a 1-mL, 0.2um PTFE 96 filter well plate (filter plate) with 2x 0.5 mL aliquots of methanol followed by 2 x 0.5 mL ultrapure water using a N2 going through a 96-well positive pressure system. The waste from the conditioning steps should be discarded. Ensure that the PTFE membranes do not dry as this will cause difficulty in filtering the samples. Once the water has been pushed through, it is difficult to see if all wells are empty, so tap plate upside down on a kimwipe to ensure that water is not present in any wells.

steps.title: Place the pre-conditioned filter plate on...
steps.instruction: Place the pre-conditioned filter plate on top of a new 1-mL 96-well collection plate.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant from each centrifuged...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant from each centrifuged sample into a single well (a template can be utilized to track sample location on the wellplate). If process blanks have been used, add them to the filter plate as well.

steps.title: Once all samples (and/or blanks) have...
steps.instruction: Once all samples (and/or blanks) have been transferred to the filter wellplate, use the 96-well positive pressure system to push the samples through the filter. Do not go higher than 9 psi or you will risk splashing as the samples go into the wellplate below the filter plate.

steps.title: Once filtering step is complete, transfer...
steps.instruction: Once filtering step is complete, transfer each sample from its well into a Waters Corp Clear Glass 12 x 32 mm Screw Neck Total Recovery Vial. Apply a non-slit autosampler vial cap with Red PTFE / White Silicone Rubber / Red PTFE Septa to the vial and store sample at -20 ℃ until needed for analysis.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: our laboratory takes a small portion of each filtered sample (amount depends upon number of samples in the batch) and pools them to create a quality control sample used to help normalize the LC-MS data. Our sample Metabolomics LC-MS/MS protocol is described in the protocols.io titled: " Protocol for the LC-MS/MS-based metabolomics analysis of samples generated using the MPLEx method ".

steps.title: Store samples at -20 ℃ until needed for LC-MS Analysis
steps.instruction: Store samples at -20 ℃ until needed for LC-MS Analysis. Our sample Lipidomics LC-MS/MS protocol is described in the Protocols.io titled " Protocol for the LC-MS/MS-based lipoidomics analysis of samples generated using the MPLEx method ".

steps.title: When sample is dry, remove membrane...
steps.instruction: When sample is dry, remove membrane and add 500 uL of chloroform to vial. Seal vial with a non-slit autosampler vial cap with Red PTFE / White Silicone Rubber / Red PTFE Septa and vortex briefly to dissolve any residue in the bottom of the vial.

steps.title: Note, due to the volatility of...
steps.instruction: Note, due to the volatility of the chloroform, this step shouldn't take more than 30-45 minutes (depending on volume, of course). Do not let samples sit dry longer than necessary to avoid oxidation of lipids.

steps.title: The protein pellets, after being rinsed...
steps.instruction: The protein pellets, after being rinsed with methanol and air dried, unless processed immediately, it is best to keep the samples at -80C until preparation. Note: We recommand sample re-solubilization in a buffer compatible with a protein digestion protocol. We recommend to use the protocol.io titled : " Sample Preparation (Urea/trypsin digestion) for Proteomics - Mammalian Organisms " after resolubilization in 50 mM NH4HCO3, pH ~8 buffer.

steps.title: For samples that are less than...
steps.instruction: For samples that are less than 200 µL, bring them up to 200 µL with water in a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Do not use any type of buffer as this will show up in the metabolite layer and could mask metabolic changes.

steps.title: Add 800 uL of cold (stored...
steps.instruction: Add 800 uL of cold (stored at -20C) 2:1 Chloroform:Methanol to the 200 µL of sample. This will create the 8:4:3 Chloroform:Methanol:Water ratio needed for the extraction. If working with multiple samples, vortex sample briefly following solvent addition before placing sample tube in ice.

steps.title: Following solvent addition to all samples,...
steps.instruction: Following solvent addition to all samples, vortex samples at highest speed for 1 minute followed by 5 minutes of rest on ice.

steps.title: Vortex samples again for 1 minute...
steps.instruction: Vortex samples again for 1 minute then centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4 ℃. Samples will be separated into three distinct layers following this step.

steps.title: Using a Pasteur pipette, transfer the...
steps.instruction: Using a Pasteur pipette, transfer the top (polar, metabolite) layer into a new tube or vial for metabolite analysis (see metabolite preparation section for which container is appropriate).

steps.title: Using a new Pasteur pipette, gently...
steps.instruction: Using a new Pasteur pipette, gently push past the protein interphase layer to the lower (non-polar, lipid) layer and transfer to a Waters Corp Clear Glass 12 x 32 mm Screw Neck Total Recovery Vial.

steps.title: Wash the protein interlayer with 1...
steps.instruction: Wash the protein interlayer with 1 mL of cold (-20 ℃) Methanol and then centrifuge again at 10,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4 ℃.

steps.title: Following centrifugation, the protein should be...
steps.instruction: Following centrifugation, the protein should be in a pellet at the bottom of the microcentrifuge tube. Gently pour the methanol off of the pellet and place the tubes upside down inside a fume hood for 5 minutes to dry. Ensure pellet doesn't dry for longer than 5 minutes or reconstitution can be an issue.
